pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 5 and export_result.sh line 4 both define OUTPUT_FILE="/sdcard/trip_duration.txt". This is the file path where the task output must be written.

Web search (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Jose,_California and https://www.britannica.com/place/San-Jose-California): San Jose is a real city in California, the third-largest city in California and the largest city in the Bay Area. Located in the Santa Clara Valley at coordinates approximately 37.287°N, 121.843°W, about 50 miles southeast of San Francisco. It is the 12th largest city in the United States with a population over 997,000 residents.

Code analysis: The task description states 'read the estimated duration (e.g., "34 min", "1 h 12 min")'. The verifier.py (line 83-86) confirms this by asking the VLM to identify 'the estimated duration displayed on the screen'. The metadata key 'target_ui_element' refers to the UI component in Sygic GPS Navigation that displays the estimated route duration. Web search results (https://www.sygic.com/what-is/route-summary) confirm Sygic displays route duration estimates on the route planning/summary screen.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires an agent to search for 'San Jose, CA' (a real city in California, USA) in the Sygic GPS Navigation application (com.sygic.aura), calculate a driving route to that destination, and extract the estimated trip duration from the route summary screen. The estimated duration string (e.g., '34 min' or '1 h 12 min') must be written to the file /sdcard/trip_duration.txt. Sygic GPS Navigation is a real, widely-used offline navigation application (200 million drivers, available on Google Play Store). The task is synthetic but uses real locations and real application features.
